assertions.expectation: Explains that GKE's maximum upgrade parallelism is approximately 20 nodes simultaneously, regardless of maxSurge setting
assertions.reason: Response suggests maxSurge controls actual parallelism without mentioning GKE's hard limit of ~20 nodes simultaneous upgrades

assertions.expectation: Calculates that at 20-node parallelism, upgrading 3,000 nodes requires ~150 batches — far more than an 8-hour window allows
assertions.reason: Response provides upgrade rate estimates (50-100 nodes/hour for CPU, 20-30 for GPU) but doesn't perform the specific 20-node parallelism calculation or batch analysis

assertions.expectation: Recommends splitting upgrades across multiple maintenance windows (weekends) rather than attempting all at once
assertions.reason: Response suggests bi-weekly Saturday + Sunday windows and acknowledges that the full upgrade cannot be completed in a single 8-hour window

assertions.expectation: Suggests upgrading CPU pools and GPU pools separately, prioritizing CPU pools first as they're less disruptive
assertions.reason: Response explicitly recommends upgrading CPU pools first and GPU pools second, noting CPU pools are typically faster

assertions.expectation: Recommends upgrading GPU pools during scheduled gaps between training/inference workloads
assertions.reason: Response doesn't mention timing GPU pool upgrades around training/inference workload schedules

assertions.expectation: Suggests expanding the maintenance window or using multiple consecutive windows for large pool upgrades
assertions.reason: Response suggests extending windows to Friday 10pm - Saturday 2pm (16 hours) and bi-weekly Saturday + Sunday options

assertions.expectation: Mentions that cluster autoscaler can only scale one node pool at a time — this interacts with upgrade scheduling
assertions.reason: Response doesn't mention the cluster autoscaler limitation or its interaction with upgrade scheduling

assertions.expectation: Addresses the 15,000 node standard limit and notes GKE 1.31+ supports up to 65,000 for AI workloads
assertions.reason: Response doesn't mention GKE node limits or the increased limits for AI workloads in newer versions
